title: 32 Claude Shortcut Hacks for Faster Prompts
description: A practical training program to master Claude prompt shortcuts and get faster, sharper, more useful AI responses every time.

modules.title: Simplify and Summarize
modules.agentInstructions: Learn to instantly control how complex or concise a response is.

steps.title: /ELI5 — Explain Like I'm 5
steps.content:
Add `/ELI5` at the start of any prompt to get a simple, jargon-free explanation.

**How to use it:**
> `/ELI5: How does compound interest work?`

**When to use it:**
- Learning a new topic fast
- Explaining something to a non-expert
- Breaking down complex documentation

**Practice:** Pick one complex topic from your work and run it through `/ELI5`. Notice how the language shifts.

steps.title: /TLDR — Summarize Long Text
steps.content:
Add `/TLDR` before a block of text to get a 2-4 line summary instantly.

**How to use it:**
> `/TLDR: [paste your long article, report, or email here]`

**When to use it:**
- Reviewing long reports
- Digesting research papers
- Summarizing meeting notes

**Practice:** Paste a long email or article and use `/TLDR`. Compare it to reading the full text.

steps.title: /BRIEFLY — Force a Short Answer
steps.content:
Use `/BRIEFLY` when you need a quick answer without explanation or filler.

**How to use it:**
> `/BRIEFLY: What is the difference between revenue and profit?`

**When to use it:**
- Quick fact checks
- When you already know the basics
- Mobile or time-pressured situations

**Practice:** Ask three questions you already know the answer to using `/BRIEFLY`. Check the accuracy and speed.

steps.title: /EXEC SUMMARY — Professional-Style Summary
steps.content:
Use `/EXEC SUMMARY` to get a crisp, boardroom-ready summary of any topic or document.

**How to use it:**
> `/EXEC SUMMARY: [paste strategy document or topic]`

**When to use it:**
- Preparing for leadership meetings
- Summarizing proposals
- Briefing stakeholders quickly

**Practice:** Take your next project brief and run it through `/EXEC SUMMARY`. Use the output in your next meeting.

modules.title: Control Tone, Style, and Audience
modules.agentInstructions: Shape how Claude speaks, who it speaks to, and what energy it brings.

steps.title: /TONE — Set the Emotional Register
steps.content:
Use `/TONE:` followed by your desired tone to shift the entire feel of a response.

**How to use it:**
> `/TONE: Funny — Write a company update about our Q3 results`
> `/TONE: Formal — Write a client apology email`

**Available tones to try:**
- Funny, Formal, Dramatic, Empathetic, Direct, Inspirational

**Practice:** Write the same message in three different tones. Notice what changes and what stays the same.

steps.title: /AUDIENCE — Adapt for Specific Readers
steps.content:
Tell Claude exactly who will read the response so it calibrates vocabulary, depth, and examples.

**How to use it:**
> `/AUDIENCE: Senior executives — Explain our new product roadmap`
> `/AUDIENCE: First-year marketing students — Explain brand positioning`

**When to use it:**
- Writing for different departments
- Creating training materials
- Pitching to different stakeholders

**Practice:** Take one piece of content and rewrite it for two completely different audiences using this shortcut.

steps.title: /REWRITE AS — Change the Style Instantly
steps.content:
Use `/REWRITE AS:` to transform existing text into a completely different style or voice.

**How to use it:**
> `/REWRITE AS: Hemingway — [paste your text]`
> `/REWRITE AS: LinkedIn thought leader — [paste your text]`
> `/REWRITE AS: Legal document — [paste your text]`

**Practice:** Take a plain email you wrote recently and rewrite it three different ways using this shortcut.

steps.title: /JARGON — Go Full Technical
steps.content:
Use `/JARGON` when you want Claude to use domain-specific, expert-level vocabulary.

**How to use it:**
> `/JARGON: Explain how neural networks learn`
> `/JARGON: Describe a go-to-market strategy`

**When to use it:**
- Writing for expert audiences
- Technical documentation
- Peer-to-peer professional communication

**Practice:** Compare a `/JARGON` response vs. an `/ELI5` response on the same topic. Study the contrast.

modules.title: Structure and Format Outputs
modules.agentInstructions: Take full control of how information is organized and presented.

steps.title: /CHECKLIST — Turn Any Response Into a Checklist
steps.content:
Convert instructions, processes, or advice into an actionable checklist.

**How to use it:**
> `/CHECKLIST: How to launch a new product`
> `/CHECKLIST: Steps to prepare for a job interview`

**When to use it:**
- Creating SOPs
- Onboarding documents
- Personal task planning

**Practice:** Take your next project kickoff and run it through `/CHECKLIST`. Use the output as your actual task list.

steps.title: /FORMAT AS — Enforce a Specific Output Format
steps.content:
Tell Claude exactly what format to deliver the answer in.

**How to use it:**
> `/FORMAT AS: Table — Compare the top 5 CRM tools`
> `/FORMAT AS: JSON — List the properties of a user profile`
> `/FORMAT AS: Bullet points — Key takeaways from this article`

**Formats to try:**
- Table, JSON, CSV, Numbered list, Markdown, HTML

**Practice:** Request the same information in three different formats. Identify which works best for your use case.

steps.title: /SCHEMA — Generate a Structured Outline or Data Model
steps.content:
Use `/SCHEMA` to build a structured framework, outline, or data architecture instantly.

**How to use it:**
> `/SCHEMA: A content marketing strategy`
> `/SCHEMA: A user database for an e-commerce app`

**When to use it:**
- Planning projects
- Designing databases
- Building content frameworks

**Practice:** Use `/SCHEMA` to outline your next presentation or project plan before writing a single word.

steps.title: /STEP-BY-STEP — Force Sequential Reasoning
steps.content:
Make Claude walk through any process or explanation in clear, numbered steps.

**How to use it:**
> `/STEP-BY-STEP: How to run a Facebook ad campaign`
> `/STEP-BY-STEP: How to negotiate a salary`

**When to use it:**
- Learning new processes
- Creating tutorials
- Solving multi-part problems

**Practice:** Use `/STEP-BY-STEP` on a process you teach others regularly. Compare it to your current documentation.

modules.title: Role-Playing and Perspective Shifting
modules.agentInstructions: Unlock multi-dimensional thinking by assigning roles and viewpoints.

steps.title: /ACT AS — Assign Claude a Specific Role
steps.content:
Tell Claude to think, speak, and respond from a specific professional or character perspective.

**How to use it:**
> `/ACT AS: A venture capitalist — Review my startup pitch`
> `/ACT AS: A skeptical customer — React to this product description`
> `/ACT AS: A Stoic philosopher — Give me advice on dealing with failure`

**Practice:** Pick a key decision you're facing. Use `/ACT AS` with three different roles (critic, supporter, neutral expert) and compare the responses.

steps.title: /MULTI-PERSPECTIVE — See All Sides
steps.content:
Ask Claude to present multiple distinct viewpoints on a single topic or question.

**How to use it:**
> `/MULTI-PERSPECTIVE: Should companies mandate return to office?`
> `/MULTI-PERSPECTIVE: Is AI a net positive for society?`

**When to use it:**
- Preparing for debates or negotiations
- Writing balanced analyses
- Making complex decisions

**Practice:** Use `/MULTI-PERSPECTIVE` on a controversial topic in your industry. Use the output to stress-test your own position.

steps.title: /PARALLEL LENSES — Examine From Multiple Angles Simultaneously
steps.content:
Similar to `/MULTI-PERSPECTIVE` but more structured — Claude examines the topic through specific analytical lenses at the same time.

**How to use it:**
> `/PARALLEL LENSES: Evaluate remote work through economic, psychological, and cultural lenses`

**Lenses to try:**
- Economic, Ethical, Technical, Cultural, Historical, Psychological

**Practice:** Take a major business decision and examine it through at least four parallel lenses.

steps.title: /COMPARE — Side-by-Side Analysis
steps.content:
Force Claude to directly compare two or more things in a structured, balanced way.

**How to use it:**
> `/COMPARE: Shopify vs. WooCommerce for a small business`
> `/COMPARE: Working in a startup vs. a corporate company`

**When to use it:**
- Vendor or tool selection
- Strategic trade-off analysis
- Research and reporting

**Practice:** Identify a real decision you need to make this week and use `/COMPARE` to lay out the options clearly.

modules.title: Advanced Reasoning and Critical Thinking
modules.agentInstructions: Push Claude beyond surface-level answers into deep, rigorous thinking.

steps.title: /FIRST PRINCIPLES — Rebuild From the Ground Up
steps.content:
Strip away assumptions and rebuild understanding from fundamental truths.

**How to use it:**
> `/FIRST PRINCIPLES: Why do most startups fail?`
> `/FIRST PRINCIPLES: What makes a great manager?`

**When to use it:**
- Challenging conventional wisdom
- Solving persistent problems
- Innovation and strategy work

**Practice:** Take a belief you hold strongly in your field and challenge it using `/FIRST PRINCIPLES`. What survives?

steps.title: /CHAIN OF THOUGHT — Show All Reasoning Steps
steps.content:
Ask Claude to reveal its full reasoning process, not just the conclusion.

**How to use it:**
> `/CHAIN OF THOUGHT: Should I raise prices for my service?`
> `/CHAIN OF THOUGHT: What's the best way to structure this team?`

**When to use it:**
- Complex decisions
- Math or logic problems
- Anywhere the reasoning matters as much as the answer

**Practice:** Compare a standard prompt response vs. a `/CHAIN OF THOUGHT` response on the same question. What did you miss?

steps.title: /DELIBERATE THINKING — Slow Down the Response
steps.content:
Force Claude to think more carefully and resist quick, obvious answers.

**How to use it:**
> `/DELIBERATE THINKING: What are the long-term risks of scaling too fast?`

**When to use it:**
- High-stakes decisions
- Strategic planning
- Any question where the first answer is probably too simple

**Practice:** Use `/DELIBERATE THINKING` on your most important current challenge. Sit with the response for 24 hours before acting.

steps.title: /PITFALLS — Identify What Could Go Wrong
steps.content:
Ask Claude to proactively surface traps, mistakes, and failure modes before you commit.

**How to use it:**
> `/PITFALLS: Launching a subscription business`
> `/PITFALLS: Hiring your first employee`
> `/PITFALLS: Running a performance review process`

**Practice:** Before your next major decision or launch, run it through `/PITFALLS`. Add the top three findings to your risk register.

steps.title: /SWOT — Instant Strategic Analysis
steps.content:
Generate a structured Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats analysis on any topic.

**How to use it:**
> `/SWOT: My freelance consulting business`
> `/SWOT: Entering the European market`

**When to use it:**
- Strategic planning sessions
- Investor or board prep
- Evaluating new opportunities

**Practice:** Run a `/SWOT` on your current role, project, or business. Be honest about what shows up in Weaknesses.

modules.title: Quality Control and Meta-Prompting
modules.agentInstructions: Use Claude to evaluate, refine, and guardrail its own outputs.

steps.title: /EVAL-SELF — Ask Claude to Critique Its Own Answer
steps.content:
After receiving a response, ask Claude to evaluate the quality of what it just said.

**How to use it:**
> `/EVAL-SELF: [paste Claude's previous response]`
> Or add it directly: `/EVAL-SELF: Explain the best pricing strategy for SaaS`

**When to use it:**
- When an answer feels too easy or generic
- Before using AI output in important work
- Training yourself to spot weak responses

**Practice:** Run any response through `/EVAL-SELF` and compare what Claude flags vs. what you noticed yourself.

steps.title: /REFLECTIVE MODE — Prompt Self-Awareness in the Response
steps.content:
Ask Claude to reflect on its own assumptions, limitations, and confidence level within the answer itself.

**How to use it:**
> `/REFLECTIVE MODE: What is the best leadership style?`

**When to use it:**
- Topics with no clear right answer
- When you suspect oversimplification
- Research and analysis work

**Practice:** Use `/REFLECTIVE MODE` on three prompts this week. Track how often it changes your confidence in the output.

steps.title: /SYSTEMATIC BIAS CHECK — Surface Hidden Assumptions
steps.content:
Ask Claude to identify what biases, assumptions, or gaps might be embedded in its response.

**How to use it:**
> `/SYSTEMATIC BIAS CHECK: What makes a successful entrepreneur?`
> `/SYSTEMATIC BIAS CHECK: [paste any analysis or piece of writing]`

**When to use it:**
- Diversity, equity, and inclusion work
- Research and reporting
- Any topic with cultural or political dimensions

**Practice:** Run three pieces of content you've already written through `/SYSTEMATIC BIAS CHECK`. What comes up?

steps.title: /NO AUTOPILOT — Ban Generic Responses
steps.content:
Explicitly tell Claude not to give the safe, predictable, surface-level answer.

**How to use it:**
> `/NO AUTOPILOT: What should a new manager focus on in their first 90 days?`

**When to use it:**
- Topics that tend to produce clichés
- When previous attempts felt generic
- Creative or strategic work where originality matters

**Practice:** Take a prompt where Claude gave you a disappointing answer previously. Add `/NO AUTOPILOT` and compare results.

steps.title: /GUARDRAIL — Set Hard Limits on the Response
steps.content:
Define explicit boundaries that Claude must not cross in its response.

**How to use it:**
> `/GUARDRAIL: Do not suggest any solution that requires a budget over $10k — How should I grow my audience?`
> `/GUARDRAIL: Only use peer-reviewed sources — Explain the benefits of intermittent fasting`

**When to use it:**
- Constrained projects
- Regulated industries
- Any time scope creep is a risk

**Practice:** Identify your most common constraints at work and build a library of `/GUARDRAIL` phrases you can reuse.

steps.title: /ROLE: TASK: FORMAT: — The Master Prompt Structure
steps.content:
Combine all three elements in one structured prompt for maximum precision and control.

**How to use it:**
> `/ROLE: Senior copywriter TASK: Write a product launch email FORMAT: Short paragraphs, max 200 words, strong CTA at the end`

**Why this works:**
This shortcut forces you to define exactly *who* is answering, *what* they are doing, and *how* the output should look — eliminating ambiguity completely.

**Practice:** Rebuild your three most-used prompts using the `/ROLE: TASK: FORMAT:` structure. Save them as templates for future use.

> **Final Tip:** You don't have to use these shortcuts one at a time. Stack them for even more powerful results.
> **Example:** `/NO AUTOPILOT + FIRST PRINCIPLES + FORMAT AS: Table — What makes a great company culture?`
>
> *Adapted from Ruben Hassid (https://ruben.substack.com/)*
